title: Formation Flight wake-core transport versus amplitude discriminator
scientificQuestion: Does a bounded spatial displacement of the c16 wake explain the late early-cycle c20 field difference, or does a substantial amplitude/diffusion residual remain after alignment?

commonGrid.solidExclusion: union of either-grid owner masks plus one c20-cell dilation

alignmentSearch.objective: minimum combined normalized squared residual energy of signed vertical velocity and vorticity magnitude in the locked wake region
alignmentSearch.normalization: each signal divided by the larger unshifted c16/c20 RMS over the common valid wake region

decisionRule.aggregateDefinition: sum unshifted and optimally shifted normalized residual energy across all five locked phases before taking the reduction fraction
decisionRule.ifDisplacementDominated: promote a localized wake-following refinement centered on the measured displacement path
decisionRule.ifAmplitudeDiffusionDominated: test collision/advection dissipation in the locked wake region before spatial refinement
decisionRule.ifMixed: retain both spatial transport and amplitude pathways in one localized discriminator

claimBoundary: Spatial registration is a numerical mechanism diagnostic. It cannot authorize the formation-power magnitude, establish biological formation benefit, or replace a refinement study.
